data.title: Trump grants two-year regulatory relief to coal, chemical, and high-pollution industries
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Thursday granted two years of regulatory relief from certain regulations for coal-fired power plants, chemical manufacturers, and other high-pollution industries. 

The move is aimed at rolling back environmental rules put in place during the Biden administration, which Trump argues are overly restrictive.

In a series of proclamations issued late Thursday, Trump granted temporary exemptions to several industries he described as critical to national security.

The proclamations cover coal-fired power plants, taconite iron ore processing facilities used to make steel, and chemical manufacturers that help produce semiconductors and medical device sterilizers.

The proclamations allow the facilities to comply with Environmental Protection Agency standards that were in place before rules imposed in recent years by President Joe Biden's administration, the White House said.

"Biden-era emissions standards impose costly and, in some cases, unattainable compliance requirements on these industries essential to national interests," the White House said in a fact sheet.

data.title: Trump's push for cane sugar in Coke could hurt U.S. farmers
China’s spy agency accuses foreign agencies of smuggling rare earths - report
data.description:
• China's Ministry of State Security on Friday said foreign spy agencies had tried to "steal" rare earths and pledged to crack down on infiltration and espionage targeted at its critical mineral sector, Reuters reported.
  • Foreign intelligence agencies and their agents had colluded with "domestic lawbreakers" to steal rare earth-related items from China, posing a serious threat to China's national security, the spy agency said in a statement on its WeChat account without naming any specific country, the report said.
  • The ministry said it had detected attempts by an unnamed country to bypass export restrictions by forging labels, falsifying cargo manifests and transshipping cargoes, where products are routed through third countries before going to their final destination.
  • Reuters earlier reported this month that unusually large quantities of antimony - a metal used in batteries, chips, and flame retardants - appeared to have been transshipped into the United States via Thailand and Mexico after China banned U.S. exports.

 

  • ETF: (NYSEARCA:REMX), (NYSEARCA:LIT), (NYSEARCA:BATT), (URNM), (NLR), (URA)

data.title: AB SKF Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 5.06, revenue of SEK 23.17B; initiates Q3 and reaffirms outlook
data.description:
• AB SKF (publ) (OTCPK:SKFRY): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 5.06.
  • Revenue of SEK 23.17B (-9.5% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted operating margin 13.3% (13.0%) with Industrial at 16.6% (16.3%) and Automotive at 5.1% (5.3%).
  • Operating margin 5.6% (9.7%).
  • Net cash flow from operating activities, SEK 2,817M.
  • Outlook:  Q3 2025: While the global economic development makes the outlook uncertain, we expect organic sales to be relatively unchanged, year-over-year.
  • Guidance Q3 2025 • Currency impact on the operating profit is expected to be around MSEK 500 negative compared to the third quarter 2024, based on exchange rates per 30 June 2025.
  • Guidance FY 2025 • Tax level excluding effects related to divested businesses: around 26%. • Additions to property, plant and equipment: around BSEK 4.5 excluding separation of the Automotive business

data.title: AI search startup Perplexity valued at $18B after new funding - report
data.description:
• Perplexity, the artificial intelligence search startup backed by Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), has reportedly raised fresh capital in a deal that values the company at $18 billion.
  • Perplexity AI raised $100 million through the financing, Bloomberg reported.
  • The deal is an extension of a previous round from a few months ago that  valued the company at $14 billion. 
  • Perplexity's AI-powered search engine competes with Google (GOOG).

data.title: AB Electrolux GAAP EPS of SEK 0.65, revenue of SEK 31.28B; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• AB Electrolux (publ) press release (OTCPK:ELUXY): Q2 GAAP EPS of SEK 0.65.
  • Revenue of SEK 31.28B (-7.5% Y/Y).
  • Operating cash flow after investments was SEK -741M.

data.title: Graco buys Color Service for €63 million
Telenor ASA reports Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Telenor ASA press release (OTCQX:TELNY): Q2 service revenues of NOK 16.5 billion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of NOK 9.3 billion.
  • Free cash flow before M&A of NOK 1.6 billion.
  • During the second quarter, Telenor delivered year-over-year organic growth of 2.9 per cent in service revenues and 8.3 per cent growth in EBITDA.

  • Financial outlook for 2025:

    • Low single-digit organic growth in Nordic service revenues.

    • High-single-digit organic growth in adjusted EBITDA in the Nordics (increased from mid-single-digit).

    • For the Nordic business, capex excl. leases are expected to account for around 14 per cent of revenues.

    • Mid-single-digit organic growth in the Group's EBITDA (increased from low-to-mid-single-digit).

    • Free cash flow before M&A of around NOK 13 billion.

data.title: Park-Ohio prices $350M senior secured notes
data.description:
• Park-Ohio Industries (NASDAQ:PKOH), a subsidiary of Park-Ohio said that it has priced its offering of $350M aggregate principal amount of senior secured notes due 2030.
  • The notes were priced at 99.500% of par and will bear an interest rate of 8.500% per annum.
  • The notes will be senior obligations of the company and will be guaranteed, with certain exceptions, by the company’s existing and future domestic subsidiaries on a senior secured basis.
  • The company expects to close the offering on July 31, 2025
  • Proceeds along with cash on hand, will be used to redeem all $350.0 million aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 6.625% senior notes due 2027.

data.title: Fed’s Waller pushes for July rate cut
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said policymakers should cut interest rates this month to support a labor market that is showing signs of weakness.

“With inflation near target and the upside risks to inflation limited, we should not wait until the labor market deteriorates before we cut the policy rate,” he said Thursday in the text of a speech prepared for an event hosted by the Money Marketeers in New York. “I believe it makes sense to cut the FOMC’s policy rate by 25 basis points two weeks from now.”

The U.S. central bank held its policy rate steady at the 4.25%-4.50% target range for a fourth straight meeting in June. The Fed's next policy meeting is scheduled for July 29-30.

“I see the hard and soft data on economic activity and the labor market as consistent: The economy is still growing, but its momentum has slowed significantly, and the risks to the (Federal Open Market Committee’s) employment mandate have increased,” and that justifies cutting rates, Waller added.

Waller is one of two officials backing a rate cut in July, arguing that the recent import tax spike is likely a one-off event that policymakers can look past. He said the Fed no longer needs a restrictive stance, with its benchmark rate well above the 3% long-run neutral level.

If inflation stays subdued and expectations of future price increases remain anchored, Waller said he would “support further 25 basis point cuts to move monetary policy toward neutral,” he said.

Waller also warned that holding off on easing this month could pose risks later. The Fed last cut rates in December 2024.

To track the U.S. economy using ETFs, investors can consider broad market ETFs: (SP500), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).
